title: Authors and Citation
text: Hester J, Csárdi G (2026). archive: Multi-Format Archive Compression Support. R package version 1.1.13.9000, https://archive.r-lib.org/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {archive: Multi-Format Archive and Compression Support},   author = {Jim Hester and Gábor Csárdi},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 1.1.13.9000},   url = {https://archive.r-lib.org/}, }

title: Multi-Format Archive and Compression Support
text: R bindings libarchive http://www.libarchive.org. Supports many archives formats, including tar, ZIP, 7-zip, RAR, CAB. Also supports many filters gzip, bzip2, compress, lzma, xz uuencoded files, among others. archive provides interfaces read write connections archives, well efficiently reading writing archives directly disk.

title: Multi-Format Archive and Compression Support
text: can install archive CRAN :

title: Multi-Format Archive and Compression Support
text: Use archive_read() archive_write() read write single files archive. files return connections, can passed R interface can take connection. base R file system functions use connections, well packages like readr.
code: library(readr) # read_csv(), write_csv(), cols()  # Write a single dataset to zip write_csv(mtcars, archive_write("mtcars.zip", "mtcars.csv"))  # Read the data back, by default the first file is read from the archive. read_csv(archive_read("mtcars.zip"), col_types = cols()) #> # A tibble: 32 × 11 #>     mpg   cyl  disp    hp  drat    wt  qsec    vs    am  gear  carb #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1  21       6   160   110  3.9   2.62  16.5     0     1     4     4 #> 2  21       6   160   110  3.9   2.88  17.0     0     1     4     4 #> 3  22.8     4   108    93  3.85  2.32  18.6     1     1     4     1 #> 4  21.4     6   258   110  3.08  3.22  19.4     1     0     3     1 #> # ℹ 28 more rows  # Also supports things like archiving and compression together # Write a single dataset to (gzip compressed) tar write_csv(mtcars, archive_write("mtcars.tar.gz", "mtcars.csv", options = "compression-level=9"))  # Read the data back read_csv(archive_read("mtcars.tar.gz"), col_types = cols()) #> # A tibble: 32 × 11 #>     mpg   cyl  disp    hp  drat    wt  qsec    vs    am  gear  carb #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1  21       6   160   110  3.9   2.62  16.5     0     1     4     4 #> 2  21       6   160   110  3.9   2.88  17.0     0     1     4     4 #> 3  22.8     4   108    93  3.85  2.32  18.6     1     1     4     1 #> 4  21.4     6   258   110  3.08  3.22  19.4     1     0     3     1 #> # ℹ 28 more rows  # Archive file sizes file.size(c("mtcars.zip", "mtcars.tar.gz")) #> [1] 742 808

title: Multi-Format Archive and Compression Support
text: archive_write_files() used create new archive multiple files disk.
code: # Write a few files to the temp directory write_csv(iris, "iris.csv") write_csv(mtcars, "mtcars.csv") write_csv(airquality, "airquality.csv")  # Add them to a new archive archive_write_files("data.tar.xz", c("iris.csv", "mtcars.csv", "airquality.csv"))  # View archive contents a <- archive("data.tar.xz") a #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>   path            size date                #>   <chr>          <int> <dttm>              #> 1 iris.csv        3716 2023-12-11 12:18:04 #> 2 mtcars.csv      1281 2023-12-11 12:18:04 #> 3 airquality.csv  2890 2023-12-11 12:18:04  # By default `archive_read()` will read the first file from a multi-file archive. read_csv(archive_read("data.tar.xz"), col_types = cols()) #> # A tibble: 150 × 5 #>   Sepal.Length Sepal.Width Petal.Length Petal.Width Species #>          <dbl>       <dbl>        <dbl>       <dbl> <chr>   #> 1          5.1         3.5          1.4         0.2 setosa  #> 2          4.9         3            1.4         0.2 setosa  #> 3          4.7         3.2          1.3         0.2 setosa  #> 4          4.6         3.1          1.5         0.2 setosa  #> # ℹ 146 more rows  # Use a number to read a different file read_csv(archive_read("data.tar.xz", file = 2), col_types = cols()) #> # A tibble: 32 × 11 #>     mpg   cyl  disp    hp  drat    wt  qsec    vs    am  gear  carb #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1  21       6   160   110  3.9   2.62  16.5     0     1     4     4 #> 2  21       6   160   110  3.9   2.88  17.0     0     1     4     4 #> 3  22.8     4   108    93  3.85  2.32  18.6     1     1     4     1 #> 4  21.4     6   258   110  3.08  3.22  19.4     1     0     3     1 #> # ℹ 28 more rows  # Or a filename to read a specific file read_csv(archive_read("data.tar.xz", file = "mtcars.csv"), col_types = cols()) #> # A tibble: 32 × 11 #>     mpg   cyl  disp    hp  drat    wt  qsec    vs    am  gear  carb #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1  21       6   160   110  3.9   2.62  16.5     0     1     4     4 #> 2  21       6   160   110  3.9   2.88  17.0     0     1     4     4 #> 3  22.8     4   108    93  3.85  2.32  18.6     1     1     4     1 #> 4  21.4     6   258   110  3.08  3.22  19.4     1     0     3     1 #> # ℹ 28 more rows

title: Multi-Format Archive and Compression Support
text: file_write() returns connection filtered one compressions encodings. file_read() reads compressed file, automatically detecting compression used.
code: # Write bzip2, uuencoded data write_csv(mtcars, file_write("mtcars.bz2", filter = c("uuencode", "bzip2")))  # Read it back, the formats are automatically detected read_csv(file_read("mtcars.bz2"), col_types = cols()) #> # A tibble: 32 × 11 #>     mpg   cyl  disp    hp  drat    wt  qsec    vs    am  gear  carb #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1  21       6   160   110  3.9   2.62  16.5     0     1     4     4 #> 2  21       6   160   110  3.9   2.88  17.0     0     1     4     4 #> 3  22.8     4   108    93  3.85  2.32  18.6     1     1     4     1 #> 4  21.4     6   258   110  3.08  3.22  19.4     1     0     3     1 #> # ℹ 28 more rows

title: Construct a new archive — archive
text: function retrieves metadata files archive, can passed archive_read() archive_write create connection read write specific file archive.

title: Construct a new archive — archive
text: file File path archive. options character() default: character(0) Options pass filter format. list available options documented options can one following forms: option=value option/value pair provided every module. Modules accept option name ignore . option option provided every module value "1". !option option provided every module NULL value. module:option=value, module:option, module:!option , corresponding option value provided modules whose name matches module. See read options available read options See write options available write options password character(1) password process archive.

title: Construct a new archive — archive
text: tibble details files archive.

title: Construct a new archive — archive
code: a <- archive(system.file(package = "archive", "extdata", "data.zip")) a #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>   path            size date                #>   <chr>          <int> <dttm>              #> 1 iris.csv         192 2017-04-28 19:55:29 #> 2 mtcars.csv       274 2017-04-28 19:55:29 #> 3 airquality.csv   142 2017-04-28 19:55:29

what: Extract contents of an archive to a directory — archive_extract

text: Extract contents archive directory

title: Extract contents of an archive to a directory — archive_extract
code: archive_extract(   archive,   dir = ".",   files = NULL,   options = character(),   strip_components = 0L,   password = NA_character_ )

title: Extract contents of an archive to a directory — archive_extract
text: archive character(1) archive filename archive object. dir character(1) Directory location extract archive contents, created exist. files character() || integer() || NULL One files within archive, specified either filename position. options character() default: character(0) Options pass filter format. list available options documented options can one following forms: option=value option/value pair provided every module. Modules accept option name ignore . option option provided every module value "1". !option option provided every module NULL value. module:option=value, module:option, module:!option , corresponding option value provided modules whose name matches module. See read options available read options See write options available write options strip_components Remove specified number leading path elements. Pathnames fewer elements silently skipped. password character(1) password process archive.

title: Extract contents of an archive to a directory — archive_extract
text: filenames extracted (invisibly).

title: Extract contents of an archive to a directory — archive_extract
text: files NULL (default) files extracted.

title: Extract contents of an archive to a directory — archive_extract
code: a <- system.file(package = "archive", "extdata", "data.zip") d <- tempfile()  # When called with default arguments extracts all files in the archive. archive_extract(a, d) list.files(d) #> [1] "airquality.csv" "iris.csv"       "mtcars.csv"     unlink(d)  # Can also specify one or more files to extract d <- tempfile() archive_extract(a, d, c("iris.csv", "airquality.csv")) list.files(d) #> [1] "airquality.csv" "iris.csv"       unlink(d)

text: archive character(1) archive filename archive object. file character(1) || integer(1) filename within archive, specified either filename position. mode character(1) description open connection (opened initially).  See section ‘Modes’ base::connections() possible values. format character(1) default: NULL archive format, one choices_rd(names(archive:::archive_formats())). Supported formats differ depending libarchive version build. filter character(1) default: NULL archive filter, one choices_rd(names(archive:::archive_filters())). Supported filters differ depending libarchive version build. options character() default: character(0) Options pass filter format. list available options documented options can one following forms: option=value option/value pair provided every module. Modules accept option name ignore . option option provided every module value "1". !option option provided every module NULL value. module:option=value, module:option, module:!option , corresponding option value provided modules whose name matches module. See read options available read options See write options available write options password character(1) password process archive.

title: Create a readable connection to a file in an archive. — archive_read
text: 'archive_read' connection file within archive read.

title: Create a readable connection to a file in an archive. — archive_read
code: a <- system.file(package = "archive", "extdata", "data.zip") # Show files in archive a #> [1] "/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/archive/extdata/data.zip"  # By default reads the first file in the archive. read.csv(archive_read(a), nrows = 3) #>   Sepal.Length Sepal.Width Petal.Length Petal.Width Species #> 1          5.1         3.5          1.4         0.2  setosa #> 2          4.9         3.0          1.4         0.2  setosa #> 3          4.7         3.2          1.3         0.2  setosa  # Can also specify a filename directly read.csv(archive_read(a, "mtcars.csv"), nrows = 3) #>    mpg cyl disp  hp drat    wt  qsec vs am gear carb #> 1 21.0   6  160 110 3.90 2.620 16.46  0  1    4    4 #> 2 21.0   6  160 110 3.90 2.875 17.02  0  1    4    4 #> 3 22.8   4  108  93 3.85 2.320 18.61  1  1    4    1  # Or by position read.csv(archive_read(a, 3), nrows = 3) #>   Ozone Solar.R Wind Temp Month Day #> 1    41     190  7.4   67     5   1 #> 2    36     118  8.0   72     5   2 #> 3    12     149 12.6   74     5   3  # Explicitly specify the format and filter if automatic detection fails. read.csv(archive_read(a, format = "zip"), nrows = 3) #>   Sepal.Length Sepal.Width Petal.Length Petal.Width Species #> 1          5.1         3.5          1.4         0.2  setosa #> 2          4.9         3.0          1.4         0.2  setosa #> 3          4.7         3.2          1.3         0.2  setosa

title: Create a writable connection to a file in an archive. — archive_write
text: 'archive_write' connection file within archive written.

title: Create a writable connection to a file in an archive. — archive_write
text: format filter NULL, set automatically based file extension given file writing automatically detected using Robust automatic format detection reading. traditional zip archives archive_write() creates connection writes data specified file directly. archive formats file size must known archive created, data first written scratch file disk added archive. scratch file automatically removed writing complete.

title: Create a writable connection to a file in an archive. — archive_write
code: # Archive format and filters can be set automatically from the file extensions. f1 <- tempfile(fileext = ".tar.gz")  write.csv(mtcars, archive_write(f1, "mtcars.csv")) archive(f1) #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   path        size date                #>   <chr>      <int> <dttm>              #> 1 mtcars.csv  1783 2026-04-12 08:49:53 unlink(f1)  # They can also be specified explicitly f2 <- tempfile() write.csv(mtcars, archive_write(f2, "mtcars.csv", format = "tar", filter = "bzip2")) archive(f2) #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   path        size date                #>   <chr>      <int> <dttm>              #> 1 mtcars.csv  1783 2026-04-12 08:49:53 unlink(f2)  # You can also pass additional options to control things like compression level f3 <- tempfile(fileext = ".tar.gz") write.csv(mtcars, archive_write(f3, "mtcars.csv", options = "compression-level=2")) archive(f3) #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   path        size date                #>   <chr>      <int> <dttm>              #> 1 mtcars.csv  1783 2026-04-12 08:49:53 unlink(f3)

what: Add files to a new archive — archive_write_dir
title: Add files to a new archive — archive_write_dir
text: archive_write_files() adds one files new archive. archive_write_dir() adds file(s) directory new archive.

title: Add files to a new archive — archive_write_dir
code: archive_write_dir(   archive,   dir,   format = NULL,   filter = NULL,   options = character(),   password = NA_character_,   ...,   recursive = TRUE,   full.names = FALSE )  archive_write_files(   archive,   files,   format = NULL,   filter = NULL,   options = character(),   password = NA_character_ )

title: Add files to a new archive — archive_write_dir
text: archive character(1) archive filename archive object. dir character(1) directory files add. format character(1) default: NULL archive format, one choices_rd(names(archive:::archive_formats())). Supported formats differ depending libarchive version build. filter character(1) default: NULL archive filter, one choices_rd(names(archive:::archive_filters())). Supported filters differ depending libarchive version build. options character() default: character(0) Options pass filter format. list available options documented options can one following forms: option=value option/value pair provided every module. Modules accept option name ignore . option option provided every module value "1". !option option provided every module NULL value. module:option=value, module:option, module:!option , corresponding option value provided modules whose name matches module. See read options available read options See write options available write options password character(1) password process archive. ... additional parameters passed base::dir. recursive logical.  listing recurse directories? full.names logical value.  TRUE, directory     path prepended file names give relative file path.     FALSE, file names (rather paths) returned. files character() One files add archive.

title: Add files to a new archive — archive_write_dir
text: 'archive' object representing new archive (invisibly). 'archive' object representing new archive (invisibly).

title: Add files to a new archive — archive_write_dir
code: if (archive:::libarchive_version() > "3.2.0") { # write some files to a directory d <- tempfile() dir.create(d) old <- setwd(d)  write.csv(iris, file.path(d, "iris.csv")) write.csv(mtcars, file.path(d, "mtcars.csv")) write.csv(airquality, file.path(d, "airquality.csv"))  # Add some to a new archive a <- archive_write_files("data.tar.gz", c("iris.csv", "mtcars.csv")) setwd(old) a  # Add all files in a directory a <- archive_write_dir("data.zip", d) a  unlink("data.zip") }

title: Construct a connections for (possibly compressed) files. — file_read
text: 'archive_read' connection (file_read()) 'archive_write' connection (file_write()) file.

title: Construct a connections for (possibly compressed) files. — file_read
text: file_write() returns writable output connection, file_read() returns readable input connection.

title: Construct a connections for (possibly compressed) files. — file_read
code: if (archive:::libarchive_version() > "3.2.0") { # Write bzip2, base 64 encoded data and use high compression write.csv(mtcars,   file_write("mtcars.bz2",     filter = c("uuencode", "bzip2"),     options = "compression-level=9"   ) )  # Read it back read.csv(file_read("mtcars.bz2"), row.names = 1, nrows = 3) unlink("mtcars.bz2") }

title: archive 1.1.13

text: CRAN release: 2024-11-08 changes.

title: archive 1.1.9
text: CRAN release: 2024-09-12 changes.

title: archive 1.1.8
text: CRAN release: 2024-04-28 archive_extract() now stop reading input file desired files extracted, instead always reading end file (#85, @allenluce).

title: archive 1.1.7
text: CRAN release: 2023-12-11 Fixed unsafe printf() format string (#95).

title: archive 1.1.6
text: CRAN release: 2023-09-18 Fixed compilation issues Big-endian platforms (#84, @barracuda156).

title: archive 1.1.5
text: CRAN release: 2022-05-06 archive now produce broken archives Windows (#72, @cielavenir).

title: archive 1.1.4
text: CRAN release: 2022-01-30 archive_write_dir() now works relative paths (#69). archive now works properly UCRT Windows R.

title: archive 1.1.3
text: CRAN release: 2021-11-30 Gábor Csárdi now maintainer.

title: archive 1.1.2
text: CRAN release: 2021-10-25 Fix UBSAN error related progress bar initialization (#55) Fix unterminated progress bars archive_write() friends (#60, @salim-b)

title: archive 1.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2021-10-13 archive_extract() now returns extracted files (invisibly) (#50) archive_extract(), archive_write_files() archive_write_dir() gain progress bars using cli package (#49) Remove uses deprecated function glue::collapse().

title: archive 1.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2021-08-05 archive_extract() gains strip_components argument strip leading pathname components desired (#27) archive(), archive_read(), archive_extract() file_read() now accept R connections addition file paths. allows things like read remote archives url() curl::curl() connection like file disk (#7)

title: archive 1.0.2
text: CRAN release: 2021-07-16 skip tests failing non-UTF-8 systems

title: archive 1.0.1
text: CRAN release: 2021-07-09 Fix encoding issues non-UTF-8 linux systems.

title: archive 1.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2021-06-30 Initial release
